metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decompose into at least two sequenced child tickets or define an explicit thin vertical-slice MVP: e.g., (1) generalize the code-review consumer to data-driven map + fail_mode (no plan-review changes), then (2) wire the generalized consumer into plan-review with file_impact scoping — so the riskiest new piece (plan-review scoping) is de-risked after the simpler refactor is proven.
findings.finding: The ticket touches ≥4 files, ≥4 architectural layers (routing schema, registry/descriptor, detector consumer, plan-review orchestration, det_floor extension), introduces new interfaces (fail_mode on routing, DET descriptor branch, generalized consumer), and carries 8 ACs — well past the G5 decomposition threshold — yet no child tickets are proposed and no thin vertical-slice MVP is called out. The plan proceeds as a horizontal big-bang across all layers simultaneously.
findings.suggested_fix: Split into: Ticket A — data-driven detector→criterion map + fail_mode in code-review only (detectors.py + routing schema + registry validation); Ticket B — DET descriptor branch + plan-review wiring + file_impact scoping + run_det_floor second phase. Ticket A has zero plan-review risk and can ship independently; Ticket B depends on A and isolates the novel scoping logic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `exec: DET` project criteria are excluded from the LLM-tier path: add an `exec: DET` guard in `exec_tier()` (return a sentinel or raise), in `route_criteria()` (skip `exec: DET` criteria), or in `load_criteria()` (exclude them from the descriptor set returned to LLM callers), so DET criteria are handled ONLY by the `run_det_floor` second phase.
findings.finding: Project `exec: DET` criteria activated via `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` will leak into the LLM-tier batch. `effective_criteria()` returns activated project ids regardless of `exec` tier; `load_criteria()` iterates those ids and builds descriptors for all of them; `route_criteria()` then calls `exec_tier(c)` which does NOT handle `exec: DET` (it falls through to `1-TURN`). Consequently `_project_criteria()` in `production_batch_runner.py` will include the project DET criterion in its `proj_single` list and pass it to the LLM batch — a rubric call with no prompt backing (only the DET descriptor's rule message). The plan specifies only that the second phase of `run_det_floor` runs these criteria; it does not specify the complementary exclusion from the LLM-tier routing path.
findings.location: ## Static P1–P9 + dynamic project-check phase (remediation, run 2) / Scope (Files: registry.py)
findings.suggested_fix: In `exec_tier()`, add a branch: `if str(crit.get('exec', '')).upper() == 'DET': return 'DET'` and in `route_criteria()`, skip criteria where `exec_tier(c) == 'DET'`. The `_project_criteria()` helper then naturally excludes them since it pulls from `route_criteria()`'s output. Also update `plan_review_assemble_criteria` to exclude DET-tier criteria from the `effective` vocabulary used to emit `include_<ID>` booleans.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `detector` routing-entry field's JSON key name, type (string), matching semantics (exact id vs. id-prefix), required/optional status, and add it to the `_validate_routing_entry` extension spec alongside `fail_mode`.
findings.finding: The `detector` field added to each DET routing entry is described only as 'a detector selector (id or id-prefix)' — its JSON key name, value type, matching semantics (exact string equality vs. prefix/startswith), and required-vs-optional status are never defined in the plan or schema. `_validate_routing_entry` currently validates only `exec`, `block_threshold`, and `default_posture`; the plan says it gains `fail_mode` validation but does not mention validating `detector`. An implementor writing `run_detectors` must invent the key name, decide whether `detector: 'rebar.builtin.security.'` means prefix-match or exact, and decide what happens when the field is absent.
findings.location: ## What — 'Data-driven map location' remediation section; `_validate_routing_entry` in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:218`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: '`detector` (string, required for `exec: DET` project criteria): the detector id or id-prefix; matching is `d.id == selector` (exact) or `d.id.startswith(selector)` (prefix) depending on whether the value ends with `.`. `_validate_routing_entry` rejects a `DET` entry missing `detector` or carrying a non-string value.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit tests for both `fail_mode` branches under abstain conditions: `fail_mode:closed` + engine-binary-absent → BLOCK (security-class regression test), and `fail_mode:open` + each of {absent binary, parse-error, timeout} → skip=PASS (AC5 guarantee).
findings.finding: The `fail_mode` parameterization of `apply_failclosed` (a changed existing behavior) has no test specified for either branch: (a) `fail_mode:closed` abstain → BLOCK (regression on the security class's existing behavior now going through a parameterized path) and (b) `fail_mode:open` abstain → skip=PASS (new behavior). AC5 explicitly states this must be preserved for absent-binary/parse-error/timeout, but no test is named for any of these failure scenarios.
findings.location: AC line: 'Fail-open is preserved on engine-binary-absent / parse-error / timeout for fail_mode:open' + 'Data-driven map location' section (apply_failclosed parameterization)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parametrized test (e.g. `@pytest.mark.parametrize`) over `(fail_mode, engine_outcome, expected_status)` covering `(closed, absent, BLOCK)`, `(open, absent, PASS)`, `(open, parse_error, PASS)`, `(open, timeout, PASS)` — these are pure in-process logic tests, no live oracle needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the two-phase run_det_floor design, the DET descriptor-without-prompt contract, and the plan-time DET scoping rules (file_impact-scoped / advisory / N/A-fail-open) in an ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or equivalent design doc update so future contributors have the rationale.
findings.finding: The plan introduces multiple novel architectural decisions — a two-phase run_det_floor (static P1–P9 + dynamic project-check), a new DET descriptor branch that skips get_prompt, and a new plan-time DET scoping contract (file_impact-scoped / advisory / N/A-fail-open) — but nowhere calls for capturing these in an ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design doc. Future maintainers extending the floor or adding new descriptor types have no canonical reference for why these boundaries exist.
findings.location: 'Static P1–P9 + dynamic project-check phase' section; 'DET fallback descriptor shape' in What; Acceptance Criteria overall
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `## Architecture note` subsection (or reference to an ADR file) in the plan that calls out these three decisions and commits to a doc artifact (e.g., `docs/adr/0042-det-two-phase-floor.md`) as a deliverable in Scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify an explicit expand-contract sequence for the `fail_mode` routing-schema addition: define the default/fallback for rows lacking the field (e.g., `closed` for `rebar.builtin.security.*` prefix, `open` otherwise), document the ordering constraint (data migration before or atomic with code change), and add an AC asserting security-class rows remain fail-closed after the migration.
findings.finding: Adding `fail_mode` to the routing schema risks silently reverting existing security-class rows to fail-open if those rows are not atomically updated to carry `fail_mode: closed` before or alongside the code change that reads the field — the plan names no data-migration step or expand-contract sequence for the routing schema, and no rollback path is described.
findings.location: 'Data-driven map location' section: '_validate_routing_entry gains fail_mode ∈ {open, closed} validation'; AC1 remediation run2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the no-`file_impact` behavior: state explicitly whether the consumer emits a non-blocking `DetResult` (advisory, visible in output) or suppresses the result entirely (no entry in the findings list) when no `file_impact` paths are declared.
findings.finding: When `file_impact` is absent, the plan says the DET invariant is 'advisory-only over the repo context' (implying a non-blocking DetResult is emitted and flows to det_advisory_findings), but also says matches 'cannot pin the match to this plan's change' and are 'not surfaced as new work' (implying suppression / no DetResult emitted). An implementer cannot determine which behavior is intended without stopping to ask.
findings.location: Plan-review consumer integration — Match-scoping without a diff section
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the match-scoping section, e.g.: 'When file_impact is absent, the consumer emits NO DetResult (the check is skipped entirely for this plan), not an advisory finding — this avoids surfacing pre-existing repo-wide violations as plan findings.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC verifying that `run_security_detectors` is preserved as a back-compat alias of `run_detectors` (expand-contract: alias lands in the same commit as the rename, old callers unaffected).
findings.finding: The rename of `run_security_detectors` → `run_detectors` (with back-compat alias) is a described deliverable with no acceptance criterion verifying BOTH that the old name is preserved as a working alias AND that the rename itself lands — the migration criterion gap means this can silently regress (old callers break if the alias is omitted).
findings.location: 'Data-driven map location' section: 'renamed to the general `run_detectors`, a thin back-compat alias kept'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: '`run_security_detectors` is aliased to `run_detectors`; a test imports and calls the old name and asserts it delegates correctly.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the scoping test to cover all three branches: (1) file_impact present + match in declared file → finding/block; (2) file_impact absent + match → advisory only, never block; (3) diff-only invariant at plan time → N/A status, fail-open.
findings.finding: The plan-review DET consumer introduces a three-way scoping behavior (file_impact-present → finding; file_impact-absent → advisory-only; inherently-diff-only invariant → N/A-fail-open), but only the file_impact-present path has a named test assertion. The advisory (no file_impact) and N/A-fail-open paths are new user-facing branches with no test specified.
findings.location: AC remediation run 1: 'Plan-review DET-invariant matches are scoped to the ticket's file_impact (or advisory when absent) — never an unscoped whole-repo block; a test asserts the scoping.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the structural placement of the `fail_mode` branch: name the function(s) that accept `fail_mode` as a parameter and describe the call-chain shape (e.g., 'a unified `run_detectors(*, fail_mode, ...)` that returns a `DetResult`-shaped dict, replacing both the old `run_security_detectors` + `apply_failclosed` call sites').
findings.finding: The `fail_mode` branching decision rule — 'open → abstain=PASS (skip), closed → abstain=BLOCK' — is stated in prose in the 'What' section, but its structural placement in the call chain is not specified. The plan says 'one code path parameterized by `fail_mode`' but does not say whether the branch lives inside `run_detectors` (the renamed function), inside a refactored `apply_failclosed`, or in the caller that dispatches between them. The existing `apply_failclosed` in `detectors.py` (lines 74-120) calls `run_security_detectors` and then applies fail-closed logic inline — a parameterized refactor must specify which of these two functions absorbs the `fail_mode` parameter and which remains a thin wrapper.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the DET descriptor's `scenario` field is populated via `det.rule.get('message')` (the raw rule dict), not `det.message` (no such property on the Detector dataclass — verified in `src/rebar/grounding/detectors/registry.py`).
findings.finding: The plan's DET descriptor shape specifies `scenario=detector rule message`, but the `Detector` dataclass has no `.message` property — the message string lives in `det.rule.get('message')` (the raw native rule dict). Accessing it as a property will raise `AttributeError` at runtime.
findings.location: ## What — 'DET fallback descriptor shape' bullet: `scenario=detector rule message`
findings.suggested_fix: In `_descriptor_from_prompt`'s DET branch: `scenario = det.rule.get('message') or det.id` — the rule-level `message` string is the human-readable description for opengrep/ast-grep detectors, and `det.id` is the safe fallback when absent (e.g. metric detectors).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the concrete source of `scenario` for the DET descriptor branch (e.g., 'the routing entry's `message` key, a free-text string the project author provides in the overlay, defaulting to the `detector` selector value if absent') so the branch can be implemented without guessing.
findings.finding: The DET fallback descriptor branch ('a `_descriptor_from_prompt` DET branch that skips `get_prompt`') sets `scenario`='detector rule `message`' — but the plan never names the concrete field on the engine_b/detector record from which `message` is drawn before a scan has run. The existing `_descriptor_from_prompt` (registry.py:333-361) reads `prompt.text`, `prompt.title`, `prompt.dimension` from the result of `get_prompt()`. For the DET branch, the plan replaces `prompt.text` with 'detector rule message', but the detector registry record (loaded by `load_registry`) exposes fields like `id`, `message`, `title`, `severity` — the exact field name and where it is read from (the `Registry` object vs. the routing entry) is unspecified.
findings.location: ## What — 'DET fallback descriptor shape' bullet; `_descriptor_from_prompt` in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:333`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: '`scenario` for the DET branch = `routing.get("message", selector)` — the project author may supply a human-readable description in the routing entry; absent, the detector selector string is used as a fallback.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `DetResult` field values (`status`, `blocking`, `finding`, `coverage`) for the no-`file_impact` advisory case, including the `coverage` keys that record the advisory scope (e.g., `{"scoped": false, "reason": "no_file_impact"}`).
findings.finding: When `file_impact` is absent, the plan says the DET invariant result is 'advisory-only over the repo context, never a hard block.' But `DetResult.status` is an unvalidated string constrained by convention to `pass | fail | abstain` (det_floor.py:73-92), and `det_advisory_findings` (det_floor.py:722-729) surfaces a result only when `status == 'fail' and not r.blocking`. The plan never specifies what `status` and `blocking` values the consumer emits in the 'advisory-only' case — a `fail/blocking=False` result would surface as an advisory finding, but so would a `fail/blocking=False` result for any other advisory check. There is no defined enum extension for the no-`file_impact` advisory variant, and no guidance on what `coverage` the result should carry.
findings.location: ## Plan-review consumer integration — 'Match-scoping without a diff' section; `DetResult` in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py:73`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
